investing.definition: Putting time or effort into something to get good results later.
investing.example: Investing time positively now saves trouble later.

positively.example: It's better to spend time positively than to scold afterwards.

negatively.definition: In a bad or harmful way.
negatively.example: Without preparation, you end up spending time negatively.

consuming.definition: Taking up a lot of time or energy.
consuming.example: Being a parent is always going to be time consuming.

pre-empting.definition: Doing something before a problem happens to prevent it.
pre-empting.example: Pre-empting trouble is better than dealing with it after.

pace.definition: The speed at which something happens.
pace.example: If you go too fast for the pace of your child, problems arise.

verbalize.definition: To put feelings or thoughts into words.
verbalize.example: Verbalize your child's feelings to help them understand.

warnings.definition: Messages that tell someone about something that will happen soon.
warnings.example: Give your child warnings about your plans.

arisen.example: Deal with problems positively after they have arisen.

predict.definition: To say what will happen before it happens.
predict.example: Learn to predict situations your child finds difficult.

potentially.definition: Possibly; with the chance of becoming real.
potentially.example: Think about potentially tricky situations in advance.

tricky.example: New situations can be tricky for young children.

mindset.definition: A way of thinking about things.
mindset.example: Writing it down helps you get into your child's mindset.

calmer.example: Understanding your child's feelings leads to a calmer time.

convenient.definition: Easy to deal with; not causing trouble.
convenient.example: As Gina slowed down, Aoife's behaviour became more convenient.

slowed.definition: Moved or acted at a less fast speed.
slowed.example: As Gina slowed down, things improved.

contact.definition: Being emotionally close and connected to someone.
contact.example: Staying in contact with your child means being present.

situations.definition: The events or conditions happening around you.
situations.example: She learned to see situations from her child's point of view.
